ogTitle: Pitt Town Bottoms vs Pleasure Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pitt Town Bottoms and Pleasure Point.
heading: Pitt Town Bottoms vs Pleasure Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pitt Town Bottoms, New South Wales and Pleasure Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pleasure Point has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Pitt Town Bottoms (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pitt Town Bottoms or Pleasure Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pitt Town Bottoms has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Pleasure Point scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pitt Town Bottoms scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Pleasure Point scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pitt Town Bottoms or Pleasure Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pleasure Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
